name: BenefitsCal information site
use_for: Official apply link for CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs, other county benefits
safe_claims: BenefitsCal is California's online portal for applying for and managing public benefits such as CalFresh, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs, and other county benefit programs.
AidAtlasCA may link users to BenefitsCal but must not submit applications for them.
caveats: Do not collect BenefitsCal credentials.
Do not automate application submission.

name: BenefitsCal public login/apply portal
safe_claims: Use as official destination after preparing the user; do not embed or automate.
caveats: Never ask for or store login credentials.

name: California CalFresh official site
safe_claims: CalFresh is California's SNAP/food assistance program.
The CalFresh Benefits Helpline is 1-877-847-3663.
caveats: Do not decide CalFresh eligibility or benefit amounts.

name: USDA/FNS SNAP State Directory — California
safe_claims: Use as an official federal directory page for California SNAP/CalFresh contact routing.
caveats: Directory pages may not include county-specific process changes.

name: GetCalFresh support and prep site
safe_claims: GetCalFresh explains the CalFresh process and directs users to BenefitsCal to apply.
GetCalFresh can support users with questions before/during/after the process, but AidAtlasCA should still cite official state/federal sources for program facts.
caveats: Partner/support site; not the final agency determination source.

name: California WIC Program home
safe_claims: California WIC provides nutrition support to eligible pregnant/postpartum/breastfeeding people, infants, and children up to age 5.
caveats: Local WIC staff officially confirm eligibility.

name: California WIC Am I Eligible?
use_for: WIC caution language and eligibility-assessment handoff
safe_claims: The WIC self-assessment can indicate that a user may be eligible.
Only local WIC office staff can officially confirm WIC eligibility.
caveats: AidAtlasCA should label WIC as 'needs more information' when pregnancy/postpartum/breastfeeding or child age facts are missing.

name: California WIC Office & Grocer Locator
use_for: Find WIC offices and authorized grocers by city/ZIP
safe_claims: Use this official locator for current WIC office/grocer information.
caveats: Do not depend on undocumented internal locator APIs.

name: DHCS Medi-Cal Apply page
safe_claims: Users can apply for Medi-Cal through BenefitsCal, Covered California, or a county office.
caveats: Do not decide Medi-Cal eligibility or plan enrollment.

name: DHCS County Offices navigator
use_for: Find county Medi-Cal offices and health coverage help
safe_claims: Use for current county Medi-Cal office routing.
caveats: Navigator may be dynamic; keep fallback county URLs in source pack.

name: Covered California Get Started
use_for: Health coverage pathway when Medi-Cal/Marketplace questions arise
safe_claims: Covered California can be used as a health coverage application pathway; if a person does not qualify for Medi-Cal, it can help with low-cost health plans.
caveats: No plan shopping or plan recommendation in MVP.

name: HealthCare.gov Content API
safe_claims: HealthCare.gov Content API exposes content objects, collections, and a site-wide index over HTTP GET.
caveats: Educational content only; not California Medi-Cal eligibility.

name: California CSD LIHEAP Program
safe_claims: LIHEAP is a federally funded program to help low-income households with heating/cooling energy costs.
California LIHEAP includes HEAP and crisis assistance components.
caveats: Funding and local providers vary; do not promise assistance.

name: CALIHEAPApply online portal
use_for: Online application link for LIHEAP/energy assistance
safe_claims: Low-income Californians can apply for LIHEAP heating/cooling assistance through this portal.
caveats: Never create accounts or submit forms for users.

name: California LifeLine
safe_claims: California LifeLine offers discounted phone service for households that qualify through program participation or income.
caveats: Do not calculate final qualification; route to official application.

name: CPUC California LifeLine Eligibility
use_for: Program participation and income-based LifeLine path
safe_claims: Program participation such as Medi-Cal, LIHEAP, SSI, and others can be a qualifying pathway; income limits also exist.
caveats: Income limits update; use source date and do not decide final eligibility.

name: CDSS County Offices
safe_claims: Use as official statewide county social services directory.
caveats: County page details can change; link to official county pages when possible.

name: CDSS Food Banks list
safe_claims: For Santa Clara County, CDSS lists Second Harvest Food Bank of Silicon Valley in San Jose.
For San Francisco County, CDSS lists San Francisco Food Bank in San Francisco.
caveats: Use food bank locators/hotlines for current pantry hours and availability.

name: California Association of Food Banks members
safe_claims: The association points people needing help now to call 2-1-1 and find a local food bank.
caveats: Not a government source; use as food-resource handoff only.

name: California Public Utilities Commission 2-1-1 Information Services
use_for: Explain 211 as statewide resource referral service
safe_claims: 2-1-1 is a free phone number for local community services, available in multiple languages, including housing, utility, food, health and crisis resources.
caveats: Coverage and service-provider databases vary by county.

name: 211 California
use_for: Free help for housing, food, and financial-aid resources
safe_claims: 211 California provides free housing, food, and financial aid resource navigation by phone/text/web.
caveats: Do not claim real-time availability unless the local 211 resource source states it.

name: U.S. Census Geocoding Services API
use_for: Normalize address/ZIP to county/state/FIPS for routing
safe_claims: Census Geocoder offers programmatic REST access and can return geographic entities for U.S. addresses.
caveats: If user gives only ZIP/city, do not over-claim exact residence; use county-level routing.

name: HUD Housing Counselor API Query Tool
use_for: Find HUD-approved housing counseling agencies by city/state or lat/lon
safe_claims: HUD's tool supports searching housing counseling agencies by agency name, city/state, or current location.
caveats: Use as a referral source; do not provide legal or housing eligibility decisions.

name: 2026 HHS Poverty Guidelines Federal Register notice
safe_claims: HHS updates poverty guidelines annually; many programs use percentage multiples of these guidelines.
caveats: Do not calculate eligibility from poverty guidelines alone.

name: Open Referral Human Services Data Specification
use_for: Internal local resource schema: organizations, services, locations, contacts, schedules
safe_claims: HSDS is a flexible data model for human services directories, including services, organizations, locations, and contacts.
caveats: HSDS is a schema, not a data source.

name: DataSF Developer Resources
use_for: Optional San Francisco open data lookup using Socrata/SODA
safe_claims: DataSF developer docs state that building with open data is easy with the Socrata Open Data API.
caveats: Socrata app tokens increase limits; unauthenticated use can be throttled.

name: Socrata SODA Consumer API docs
use_for: General SODA API guidance for open data endpoints
safe_claims: Every Socrata dataset has a built-in SODA API endpoint accessible through dataset export/API controls.
caveats: Use low-rate caching and cite dataset pages.

name: Santa Clara County Apply for Public Benefits
use_for: Santa Clara County public-benefits application methods and contact info
safe_claims: Santa Clara County lists phone (408) 758-3800, fax (408) 295-9248, PO Box 11018, San Jose, CA 95103 for public benefits application support.
caveats: Do not apply or upload documents for users.

name: Santa Clara County CalFresh Food
use_for: County-specific CalFresh guidance and scam warnings
safe_claims: Use as county-specific CalFresh source; warn users not to share EBT card/PIN with callers/texts/emails.
caveats: Do not handle EBT numbers/PINs.

name: Santa Clara County General Assistance
use_for: Cash assistance prep for adults without dependent children
safe_claims: Santa Clara County GA is for adults without dependent children who have no other means of support; county eligibility worker determines eligibility.
caveats: Do not decide GA eligibility.

name: Santa Clara County WIC apply/contact
use_for: Santa Clara County WIC contact and local path
safe_claims: Santa Clara County WIC lists phone (408) 792-5101 and text (888) 413-2698 to check if a person qualifies.
caveats: Local WIC staff determine eligibility.

name: Santa Clara County WIC provider contacts
use_for: Additional WIC local agencies: Indian Health Center and Gardner Health
safe_claims: Santa Clara County lists Indian Health Center at (408) 960-0900 and Gardner Health Services at (408) 254-5197 as WIC-related contacts.
caveats: Call to confirm locations and appointment availability.

name: 211 Bay Area — Santa Clara County
use_for: Local resource handoff for Santa Clara County
safe_claims: 211 Bay Area provides free 24/7 assistance in Santa Clara County across housing, food, mental health, crisis, transportation, health care and related categories.
caveats: Use as handoff, not a scraped database.

name: City of San José Homeless Services Guide
use_for: San José homelessness and shelter handoff
safe_claims: The City of San José states that most shelters in Santa Clara County, including San José, require referral through Here4You at 408-385-2400.
caveats: Call before going; no live bed availability claims.

name: Santa Clara County Office of Supportive Housing: Temporary and Emergency Shelter
safe_claims: Santa Clara County directs people needing shelter to the Here4You hotline at (408) 385-2400.
caveats: No live shelter bed claims; instruct to call.

name: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley Get Food
jurisdiction: Santa Clara County / San Mateo County
use_for: Santa Clara food bank locator and food hotline handoff
safe_claims: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley provides a food locator for free groceries and prepared meals.
caveats: Call or use locator for current pantry availability.

name: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley Contact / Food Connection Hotline
jurisdiction: Santa Clara County / San Mateo County
safe_claims: Second Harvest lists Food Connection Hotline 1-800-984-3663 and foodconnection@shfb.org.
caveats: Not a government agency; use for food-access handoff.

name: City of San José Food and Family Resources
safe_claims: San José points users to Second Harvest at 1-800-984-3663 and 211 after hours for food and family resources.
caveats: Call before going to specific food distribution sites.

name: Law Foundation of Silicon Valley housing help
use_for: Housing legal aid and Homelessness Prevention System handoff
safe_claims: Law Foundation routes eligible low-income Santa Clara County residents at risk of homelessness to HPS assistance; contact preventhomelessness.org, (408) 516-5100, or info@preventhomelessness.org.
caveats: Do not provide legal advice; route to legal aid.

name: Bay Area Legal Aid Santa Clara County office
safe_claims: Bay Area Legal Aid Santa Clara office address: 4 North Second Street, Suite 600, San Jose, CA 95113; phone: (408) 283-3700.
caveats: Do not provide legal advice; route to legal aid.

name: San Francisco Human Services Agency
use_for: San Francisco public benefits and service-center routing
safe_claims: SFHSA offers CalFresh, CalWORKs, Medi-Cal, CAAP, child care, financial assistance, food, health, housing and jobs information.
caveats: Use specific program pages for detailed contacts.

name: SFHSA BenefitsCal page
safe_claims: San Francisco says BenefitsCal can be used to apply for benefits, upload documents, renew cases, update information, download proof of benefits, and view EBT balance.
caveats: Do not handle credentials or case data.

name: SFHSA Apply for CalFresh
safe_claims: SFHSA says users can apply for CalFresh on BenefitsCal, call (855) 355-5757, or download multilingual applications.
caveats: Do not apply or submit documents for user.

name: SFHSA Medi-Cal
safe_claims: SFHSA lists Medi-Cal contact phone (855) 355-5757, email sfbncfmc@sfgov.org, fax (415) 355-2300, BenefitsCal online, and service centers including 1460 Mission, 1440 Harrison, 3120 Mission, and 2 Gough for older adults/people with disabilities.
caveats: Do not decide Medi-Cal eligibility.

name: SFHSA Apply for Medi-Cal
safe_claims: SFHSA lists email SFMedi-Cal@sfgov.org, fax (415) 355-2432, mail PO Box 7988, San Francisco, CA 94120, and service-center drop boxes for applications.
caveats: Do not collect or transmit documents in MVP.

name: SFHSA County Adult Assistance Programs (CAAP)
safe_claims: CAAP provides cash assistance and employment services to low-income San Franciscans with no dependent children.
caveats: Do not decide CAAP eligibility.

name: SFHSA Apply for CAAP
safe_claims: SFHSA says users can apply for CAAP online at BenefitsCal or in person at 1460 Mission Street; an intake interview is scheduled.
caveats: No automatic application submission.

name: SFHSA Check CAAP Eligibility
safe_claims: San Francisco CAAP eligibility screening considers residency, income, expenses, property, vehicle ownership, student status, and CalFresh/Medi-Cal connection.
caveats: Do not calculate or decide CAAP eligibility; use to ask missing facts and route to SFHSA.

name: SFHSA Apply for CalWORKs
safe_claims: SFHSA says CalWORKs applications can start at BenefitsCal and involve a CalWORKs appointment; phone (855) 557-5100; in person at 170 Otis Street.
caveats: Do not decide CalWORKs eligibility.

name: San Francisco WIC program
safe_claims: San Francisco WIC has clinic locations and provides breastfeeding/nutrition support.
caveats: Use clinic-hours page and official WIC locator for current locations/hours.

name: San Francisco WIC clinic hours and locations
safe_claims: San Francisco lists WIC clinics including Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital WIC Clinic at 2550 23rd Street, Building 9, Room 125; phone (628) 206-5494.
caveats: Call before visiting; use official page for all clinics.

name: SFHSA Free Food Locations
use_for: San Francisco free food sources and call-before-going warning
safe_claims: SFHSA provides a free food directory and warns users to call before visiting to confirm food availability and hours.
caveats: Do not claim availability without calling or source confirmation.

name: SF-Marin Food Bank Find Food locator
use_for: San Francisco food locator and multilingual food assistance
safe_claims: SF-Marin Food Bank's locator can find free groceries, senior food boxes, emergency food, and CalFresh help; it is available in multiple languages.
caveats: Not a government agency; use as food-access handoff.

name: San Francisco Department of Homelessness and Supportive Housing
use_for: Homelessness services overview and official entry point
safe_claims: SF HSH provides information for people at risk of or experiencing homelessness, including shelter, housing, meals, clothing, and eviction prevention resources.
caveats: Do not claim shelter availability.

name: San Francisco Coordinated Entry / Access Points
safe_claims: Access Points are community gateways into San Francisco's Homelessness Response System, serving adults, families, and young adults ages 18 to 24.
caveats: Call/visit access point; no live availability claims.

name: SF Service Guide Access Point entry
use_for: Concrete adult/family/youth access point contact details
safe_claims: SF Service Guide lists an Adult/Family/Youth Coordinated Entry Access Point and contact email/phone, including 415-487-3300 x7000.
caveats: Verify current hours before routing users.

name: SF Service Guide Adult Shelter Reservation System
use_for: Adult shelter line and HSH contact
safe_claims: SF Service Guide lists SFHOT/Adult Shelter Access Line at (628) 652-8000 and HSH phone (628) 652-7700.
caveats: Call before going; no live bed availability claims.

name: 211 Bay Area Housing & Homeless Services
safe_claims: 211 Bay Area says users can dial 211 for personalized housing assistance, free/confidential/24/7 and multilingual.
caveats: Use handoff; do not scrape database.

name: Eviction Defense Collaborative
use_for: San Francisco eviction legal assistance and rent help handoff
safe_claims: EDC says people served a Summons & Complaint or notice to quit should call (415) 659-9184 or email legal@evictiondefense.org.
caveats: Do not provide legal advice.

name: San Francisco tenant and landlord resources
safe_claims: San Francisco directs tenants with eviction court papers to contact Eviction Defense Collaborative immediately.
caveats: Do not give legal advice.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
safe_claims: 988 offers call, text, and chat support for mental health struggles, emotional distress, alcohol/drug use concerns, or needing someone to talk to.
caveats: In imminent danger, advise 911/emergency services. AidAtlasCA is not crisis counseling.

name: SAMHSA National Helpline
use_for: Substance use / mental health treatment referral handoff
safe_claims: SAMHSA National Helpline is free, confidential, 24/7/365, in English and Spanish; 1-800-662-HELP (4357).
caveats: AidAtlasCA should not provide treatment advice.

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
safe_claims: The Hotline offers confidential support 24/7/365.
caveats: Do not log sensitive location details for DV situations; provide safe-exit guidance only at high level.

name: California Youth Crisis Line
safe_claims: California Coalition for Youth operates a Youth Crisis Line resource for youth in crisis.
caveats: Do not serve minors with detailed benefit workflows without guardian/professional safety logic.

name: Alameda County Social Services Agency
use_for: Alameda County public benefits office routing
safe_claims: Use as Alameda County official social services routing; link out rather than collecting application credentials.
caveats: Program pages and office details can change; pair with BenefitsCal and CDSS statewide directories.

name: Contra Costa County Employment & Human Services Department
use_for: Contra Costa County public benefits office routing
safe_claims: Use as Contra Costa County official social services routing; link out rather than collecting application credentials.
caveats: Program pages and office details can change; pair with BenefitsCal and CDSS statewide directories.

name: San Mateo County Human Services Agency
use_for: San Mateo County public benefits office routing
safe_claims: Use as San Mateo County official Human Services Agency routing; link out rather than collecting application credentials.
caveats: Program pages and office details can change; pair with BenefitsCal and CDSS statewide directories.

name: Solano County Health and Social Services
use_for: Solano County public benefits and health/social services routing
safe_claims: Use as Solano County official Health and Social Services routing; link out rather than collecting application credentials.
caveats: Program pages and office details can change; pair with BenefitsCal and CDSS statewide directories.

name: Sonoma County Human Services Economic Assistance
safe_claims: Use as Sonoma County official Economic Assistance routing; link out rather than collecting application credentials.
caveats: Program pages and office details can change; pair with BenefitsCal and CDSS statewide directories.

name: 211 Bay Area Marin County
use_for: Marin County local resource navigation by 211
safe_claims: Use as a county-specific 211 Bay Area handoff; call 211 for current local resource navigation.
caveats: Do not claim live availability from 211 directory pages.

name: 211 Bay Area Napa County
use_for: Napa County local resource navigation by 211
safe_claims: Use as a county-specific 211 Bay Area handoff; call 211 for current local resource navigation.
caveats: Do not claim live availability from 211 directory pages.

name: 211 Bay Area San Mateo County
use_for: San Mateo County local resource navigation by 211
safe_claims: Use as a county-specific 211 Bay Area handoff; call 211 for current local resource navigation.
caveats: Do not claim live availability from 211 directory pages.

name: 211 Bay Area Solano County
use_for: Solano County local resource navigation by 211
safe_claims: Use as a county-specific 211 Bay Area handoff; call 211 for current local resource navigation.
caveats: Do not claim live availability from 211 directory pages.

name: Alameda County Community Food Bank Get Food
use_for: Alameda County food locator and food assistance handoff
safe_claims: Use the Alameda County Community Food Bank get-food page as a local food handoff.
caveats: Food pantry schedules and availability can change; call before going.

name: Food Bank of Contra Costa and Solano Find Food
use_for: Contra Costa and Solano food locator handoff
safe_claims: Use the Food Bank of Contra Costa and Solano food-by-city page as a local food handoff.
caveats: Food pantry schedules and availability can change; call before going.

name: Community Action of Napa Valley Food Bank
safe_claims: Use Community Action of Napa Valley Food Bank as a local food handoff.
caveats: Food pantry schedules and availability can change; call before going.

name: Redwood Empire Food Bank Find Food
safe_claims: Use Redwood Empire Food Bank find-food resources as a local food handoff.
caveats: Food pantry schedules and availability can change; call before going.

name: CDSS CalWORKs
safe_claims: CDSS describes CalWORKs as cash aid and services for California families in need, operated locally by county welfare departments in all 58 counties.
caveats: Do not determine eligibility or estimate monthly assistance amounts.

name: CDSS In-Home Supportive Services
safe_claims: CDSS describes IHSS as in-home assistance for aged, blind, and disabled individuals as an alternative to out-of-home care.
caveats: Do not decide IHSS eligibility, authorized services, hours, rates, or medical need.

name: CDSS Child Care and Development Division
use_for: Child care support preparation and official program overview
safe_claims: CDSS describes California child care and development programs as part of a statewide child care and development system.
caveats: Do not claim subsidy eligibility or provider availability.

name: California Department of Education School Nutrition
use_for: School meals and child nutrition preparation handoff
safe_claims: The California Department of Education maintains school nutrition program information for California schools.
caveats: Do not claim a specific school meal status; route families to the school or district for current participation details.

name: California Courts Self-Help Guide
use_for: General legal information and self-help-center handoff
safe_claims: California Courts provides self-help information, forms, and self-help center routing.
caveats: Do not provide legal advice or interpret what a user should do in a case.
